steps.title: Track the causal chain
steps.content: The hypothesis links fewer dusky sharks to fewer oysters through cownose rays: sharks eat rays, and rays eat oysters. To support the hypothesis, the evidence should show that as sharks declined, rays became more abundant.

steps.title: Test each support claim
steps.content: Choice A explains other shark prey, not the ray-oyster chain. Choice B weakens the idea that rays reduce oysters. Choice C gives the wrong timing because oyster consumption increased before the shark decline. The finding that rays increased as sharks decreased supplies the missing link. The answer is D.
